stem: A healthcare study measures average patient recovery rate across 5 hospitals, each containing multiple departments. hospital A has 10 observations with mean 41.1 % (within-hospital SD = 8.5). hospital B has 10 observations with mean 33.9 % (within-hospital SD = 6.2). hospital C has 5 observations with mean 65.5 % (within-hospital SD = 5.9). hospital D has 10 observations with mean 40.9 % (within-hospital SD = 3.8). hospital E has 8 observations with mean 40.8 % (within-hospital SD = 3.6). The between-hospital standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 8.1 and 20.2 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average patient recovery rate for hospital B, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-hospital variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for hospital B: 34.1 % (shrunk from raw 33.9). Range [34.0, 34.4] as between-hospital SD varies from 8.1 to 20.2. Pooled mean: 42.2.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 42.2 % (weighted average of 5 hospital means).
Shrinkage factor for hospital B: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/10). At σ_between=13.5: B = 0.979.
Interval [34.0, 34.4] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (33.9) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (42.2) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
